message:
fix(random): correct binomial BTPE algorithm and simplify ziggurat samplers

Binomial distribution (BTPE algorithm):
- Fix fm parameter calculation: use (n+1)*r instead of n*r+r, matching
  the original Kachitvichyanukul & Schmeiser paper. The prior form
  could produce off-by-one mode estimates for certain (n, p) pairs.
- Restructure the acceptance test: swap the branch order so small-k
  cases (direct ratio test, k <= 20) are checked first, with the
  Stirling approximation path handling large k. The prior code had the
  conditions inverted, applying the expensive Stirling path to small k
  and the cheap ratio test to large k.
- Simplify the Stirling acceptance check by using the first-order
  1/(12x) correction term instead of the full 7th-order series
  expansion (13680−462−132−99−140/f²...). The higher-order terms
  contribute < 1e-10 for k values that reach this path (k > 20) and
  the simpler form matches NumPy's reference implementation.
- Add p <= 0.5 symmetry flip on return: when p > 0.5, the algorithm
  samples from the reflected distribution and must return n−y, not y.

Ziggurat normal sampler:
- Defer sign determination: generate the sign bit via a separate RNG
  call at the point of return rather than extracting it from the same
  u64 used for the index and rectangle test. This avoids a subtle
  correlation between the sign and the position within the rectangle.
- Tail sampling: use self.next_f64().ln() instead of
  (-self.next_f64()).ln_1p() for Marsaglia's tail method — the ln_1p
  form was computing ln(1 + (-U)) = ln(1-U), but the standard
  Marsaglia tail requires -ln(U) which is correctly expressed as
  .ln() on the raw uniform.

Ziggurat exponential sampler:
- Simplify bit extraction: use the same idx = r & 0xFF, rabs = r >> 8
  pattern as the normal sampler for consistency.
- Tail sampling: use .ln() instead of (-x).ln_1p() for the same
  mathematical correctness reason as above.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
